name: Endothelial telomere dysfunction induced senescence and vascular-metabolic impairment in mice
summary: Endothelial-specific telomere dysfunction provides causal animal evidence linking telomere damage to vascular and metabolic aging phenotypes.
statement: Genetic induction of telomere dysfunction specifically in mouse endothelial cells induced senescence and worsened vascular and metabolic aging phenotypes.
population_or_model: Mouse endothelial-specific model of telomere dysfunction
quantitative_note: The source reports impaired endothelial function, impaired vasodilation to insulin, reduced perfused capillary density, and increased inflammation in metabolically active tissues.
caveats: This is causal biology from a genetic dysfunction model, not evidence that an intervention preserves capping in humans.
The animal model supports mechanism and target relevance more than therapeutic readiness.
